title: Musicianship
tagline: Understand music itself — theory-first, applied to guitar and piano.

tracks.name: Theory Core
tracks.blurb: The backbone of the suite: pitch, notation, rhythm, harmony, counterpoint, and analysis, built up in order so every later idea rests on an earlier one.

lessons.title: Pitch & the Chromatic Scale
lessons.summary: Note names, sharps and flats, octaves, and the twelve-tone chromatic scale.

lessons.title: Notation & Clefs
lessons.summary: The staff, treble and bass clefs, ledger lines, and reading pitch from the page.

lessons.title: Rhythm & Meter
lessons.summary: Note durations, rests, time signatures, beat, and how meter groups pulse.

lessons.title: Score Conventions
lessons.summary: Dynamics, articulations, repeats, tempo marks, and how to read a full score.

lessons.title: Intervals
lessons.summary: Measuring distance between pitches: quality, number, and how intervals sound.

lessons.title: The Major Scale & Circle of Fifths
lessons.summary: Building major scales, key signatures, and the circle of fifths as a map of keys.

lessons.title: Minor Scales
lessons.summary: Natural, harmonic, and melodic minor, and relative vs parallel relationships.

lessons.title: Modes, Pentatonic & Blues
lessons.summary: The seven diatonic modes plus pentatonic and blues scales and their colours.

lessons.title: Solfège & Sight-Singing
lessons.summary: Movable-do solfège and a method for singing melodies at sight.

lessons.title: Triads
lessons.summary: Major, minor, diminished, and augmented triads, and their inversions.

lessons.title: Seventh Chords
lessons.summary: The five common seventh-chord qualities and how the seventh changes colour.

lessons.title: Diatonic Harmony & Roman Numerals
lessons.summary: The chords of a key and Roman-numeral analysis for labelling them.

lessons.title: SATB Voice Leading
lessons.summary: Four-part writing rules: spacing, doubling, and smooth voice motion.

lessons.title: Cadences & Phrase Structure
lessons.summary: Authentic, plagal, half, and deceptive cadences and how phrases close.

lessons.title: Non-Chord Tones
lessons.summary: Passing tones, neighbours, suspensions, and other melodic embellishments.

lessons.title: Harmonic Progression
lessons.summary: How chords tend to move: functional families and common progressions.

lessons.title: Secondary Dominants
lessons.summary: Tonicizing chords other than the tonic with applied dominant harmony.

lessons.title: Modulation
lessons.summary: Changing key mid-piece via pivot chords and other modulation techniques.

lessons.title: Modal Mixture
lessons.summary: Borrowing chords from the parallel mode for added colour.

lessons.title: Chromatic Harmony
lessons.summary: Neapolitan and augmented-sixth chords and other chromatic resources.

lessons.title: Extended & Altered Jazz Harmony
lessons.summary: Ninths, elevenths, thirteenths, alterations, and jazz chord symbols.

lessons.title: Species Counterpoint
lessons.summary: Writing independent melodic lines through the five species.

lessons.title: Figured Bass & Fugue
lessons.summary: Realizing figured bass and the architecture of a fugue.

lessons.title: Form & Analysis
lessons.summary: Binary, ternary, rondo, and sonata forms as large-scale structure.

lessons.title: Analyzing a Piece
lessons.summary: Putting the whole toolkit together to analyze a complete short work.

lessons.title: Ear Training
lessons.summary: Recognizing intervals, chords, and progressions by ear with a study plan.

tracks.name: Guitar Application
tracks.blurb: Map the theory onto six strings: the fretboard, reading on guitar, the CAGED system, and connecting positions.

lessons.title: Mapping the Fretboard
lessons.summary: Finding every note on the neck and seeing octave and string patterns.

lessons.title: Reading Music on Guitar
lessons.summary: Connecting standard notation to fret positions in first position and beyond.

lessons.title: The CAGED System
lessons.summary: Five movable chord and scale shapes that cover the whole neck.

lessons.title: Connecting Scale Positions
lessons.summary: Linking scale shapes across positions to play freely up the neck.

lessons.title: Technique: PIMA & Picking
lessons.summary: Right-hand fingerstyle (PIMA) and pick technique fundamentals.

lessons.title: Chord Construction on Guitar
lessons.summary: Building and voicing chords on the neck from their theory formulas.

tracks.name: Piano Application
tracks.blurb: The keyboard as a visual theory laboratory: geography, the grand staff, scales, and chord voicings.

lessons.title: Keyboard Geography
lessons.summary: Black-key groups, white-key names, and finding any note on the keyboard.

lessons.title: Reading the Grand Staff
lessons.summary: Treble and bass clefs together and reading both hands at once.

lessons.title: Scales & Arpeggios
lessons.summary: Fingering major and minor scales and arpeggios across the keyboard.

lessons.title: Chord Voicings & Inversions
lessons.summary: Playing triads and sevenths in root position and inversions.

lessons.title: Piano as a Theory Visualizer
lessons.summary: Using the keyboard layout to see intervals, chords, and harmony at a glance.

tracks.name: Repertoire
tracks.blurb: Theory-first breakdowns of well-known example pieces, chosen to exercise the concepts from the Theory Core on real music.

lessons.title: Example Piece: "Sadness and Sorrow"
lessons.summary: A gentle minor-key arpeggio study exercising minor scales and diatonic harmony.

lessons.title: Example Piece: Fingerstyle Intro
lessons.summary: An open-string fingerstyle intro exercising PIMA technique and triads.

lessons.title: Example Piece: Travis-Picking Study
lessons.summary: A Travis-picking study exercising the major scale and descending bass lines.

lessons.title: Example Piece: Minor Ballad
lessons.summary: A minor ballad exercising modal mixture and cadence types.

lessons.title: Example Piece: Riff & Loop
lessons.summary: A repeating minor riff exercising harmonic progression and ear training.

tracks.name: Practice System
tracks.blurb: The scaffolding that turns study into skill: routines, metronome work, sight-reading plans, and ear-training drills.

lessons.title: Building Practice Routines
lessons.summary: Structuring focused practice sessions with goals, blocks, and review.

lessons.title: Metronome Work
lessons.summary: Using a metronome to build steady time and gradually raise tempo.

lessons.title: Sight-Reading Drill Plan
lessons.summary: A graded daily plan for improving reading at sight.

lessons.title: Ear-Training Routine
lessons.summary: A repeatable routine for interval, chord, and progression recognition.
